subject: Gaming
title: Minecraft in the Classroom
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Minecraft in the Classroom.

hook.text: Minecraft is used in thousands of schools. Students learn math, history, and coding by building virtual worlds.

buildup.text: Microsoft launched Minecraft Education Edition in 2016 with lesson plans and classroom management tools.

discovery.text: Students rebuilt ancient Rome, modeled molecules, and learned programming logic—all through gameplay.

twist.text: Research shows game-based learning increases engagement and retention. Students remember what they build.

climax.text: Minecraft proved that play and learning aren't opposites. The best education feels like a game.

punchline.text: Build ancient Rome in Minecraft. Learn more than any textbook teaches.

quiz.question: How does Minecraft Education Edition benefit learning?
options: Game-based learning increases engagement and students remember what they build
